class Solution {
public:
    long long smallestNumber(long long num) {
        if (num == 0) return 0;
        string s;
        if (num > 0) {
            s = to_string(num);
            sort(s.begin(), s.end());
            // check leading zero
            if (s[0] == '0') {
                // find first non-zero
                for (int i=1; i<s.size(); ++i) {
                    if (s[i] != '0') {
                        swap(s[0], s[i]);
                        break;
                    }
                }
            }
            return stoll(s);
        } else {
            // negative
            long long abs_num = -num;
            s = to_string(abs_num);
            sort(s.begin(), s.end(), greater<char>());
            return -stoll(s);
        }
    }
};

class Solution {
public:
    long long smallestNumber(long long num) {
        if (num == 0) return 0;
        string s;
        if (num > 0) {
            s = to_string(num);
            sort(s.begin(), s.end());
            if (s[0] == '0') {
                int i = 1;
                while (i < s.size() && s[i] == '0') ++i;
                if (i < s.size()) swap(s[0], s[i]);
            }
            return stoll(s);
        } 
        else {
            // negative
            s = to_string(-num);
            sort(s.begin(), s.end(), greater<char>());
            return -stoll(s);
        }
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    long long smallestNumber(long long num) {
        if (num == 0) return 0;
        string s;
        if (num > 0) {
            s = to_string(num);
            sort(s.begin(), s.end());
            if (s[0] == '0') {
                int i = 1;
                while (i < s.size() && s[i] == '0') ++i;
                if (i < s.size()) swap(s[0], s[i]);
            }
            return stoll(s);
        } 
        else {
            s = to_string(-num);
            sort(s.begin(), s.end(), greater<char>());
            return -stoll(s);
        }
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution converts the number to a string to manipulate digits. For positive numbers, digits are sorted in ascending order, and leading zeros are fixed by swapping the first non-zero digit to the front. For negative numbers, digits are sorted in descending order to maximize the absolute value. The approach is efficient due to minimal operations and leverages sorting for optimal digit arrangement.
